items.title: CVE-2025-71347: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect malicious pickle files using numpy.f2py.crackfortran.param_eval function in red
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect malicious pickle files using numpy.f2py.crackfortran.param_eval function in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling arbitrary code execution in applications loading untrusted pickle data.

items.title: fast-mcp-telegram: Bearer token path traversal bypasses reserved Telegram session protection
items.description:
## Summary

fast-mcp-telegram validates HTTP Bearer tokens by joining the raw token string into a session-file path. The verifier rejects the exact reserved token `telegram`, but it does not reject path separators or normalize the path before checking whether the session file exists. A remote HTTP client can therefore authenticate as the default legacy session with a token such as `../fast-mcp-telegram/telegram` when the documented default session file `~/.config/fast-mcp-telegram/telegram.session` exists.

This bypasses the reserved session name control that is intended to prevent HTTP multi-user sessions from colliding with the default stdio or legacy account. With account-prefixed MCP tools enabled, the attacker still sees and calls the prefixed tools for the default account, so the prefix middleware does not stop the session selection bypass.

## Impact

An unauthenticated network client can access the Telegram account represented by the default `telegram.session` file without know
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 9.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: golang.org/x/image/tiff has excessive resource consumption in PackBits decompression
items.description: The TIFF decoder does not place a limit on the size of PackBits-compressed data. A maliciously-crafted image can exploit this to cause a small image (both in terms of pixel width/height and encoded size) to make the decoder decode large amounts of compressed data.
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: CVE-2025-71343: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect malicious pickle files that exploit lib2to3.pgen2.pgen.ParserGenerator.make_lab
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect malicious pickle files that exploit lib2to3.pgen2.pgen.ParserGenerator.make_label function in the reduce method. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files with embedded code that evades detection but executes arbitrary commands when pickle.load() is called.

items.nvd_disclosure: This CVE ID was not found in the NIST NVD database at time of enrichment. Intelligence is preliminary and unverified by NVD. CVSS score and severity are analyst-estimated until NVD confirmation.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-58522 Relative path traversal in Microsoft Edge for Android allows an u
id: db9e2d3e-464e-5ffa-9dca-ea83a75d6e7e
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-58522 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: PATHTRAVERSAL
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-58522
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/05
modified: 2026/07/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-58522 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-05T21:10:04Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-58522", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-58522 Exploitation Attempt - PATHTRAVERSAL"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3077765; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-05, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — Source 'nvd_cve' is authoritative (government/standards body)
